query: Where can I change my address, phone number or bank details as a student?

queries: Where can I change my address, phone number or bank details as a student?

retrieved.title: Change of personal data
retrieved.text:
During your studies, some of your personal data might change (e.g. your address or academic degree). It may also happen that some of your personal data in our system are incorrect (e.g. date of birth, spelling of your name).

- You can change certain data yourself via u:space, while other changes require you to provide documentation.
- Data changes are only made for active students and persons who are in the process of applying for admission.
- Doctoral/PhD students who would like to change their personal data should contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
- If you are attending a university course and would like to change your personal data, please contact the [Service Center of the Postgraduate Cente](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/)r.

You can change the following data yourself via [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast).

- Forwarding e-mail address
- Phone number
- Home and different delivery address
- Bank account details

Please note that some of these fields are required fields. Bear in mind to keep your data up to date.

If your name has changed (e.g. through marriage), please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable documentation (e.g. marriage certificate) and a scan of your passport or ID card. As soon as your has been changed, you can order a new [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal Matters > My u:card.

retrieved.title: Change of personal data
retrieved.text:
If you want your academic degree(s) that were awarded by a different university/higher education institution to appear on all your documents, please send the applicable documentation (e.g. official notice of degree award) via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office. We can only enter degrees awarded by academic institutions within the EU/EEA or degrees recognised through a [nostrification](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/) procedure. Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=ssc) of your degree programme, if an academic degree awarded by the University of Vienna does not appear in your personal data in u:space.

If you changed your citizenship, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (proof of citizenship and passport or ID card).

If your gender has been entered incorrectly, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (passport or ID card).

[Information about change of name and person for trans\*persons](http://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/aufgaben/namens-und-personenstandsaenderungen-fuer-transpersonen/)

In case your date of birth is incorrect or your name is misspelled in our system, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing documentation for the correct spelling or date of birth (passport or ID card).

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text: #### Teacher Education programme: Change of a teaching subject This kind of change is not possible via u:space. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the [deadline](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/) (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). The Admission Office will effectuate the change within the admissio period, which you will see after a few days in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".

Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the new degree programme is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March).

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again. Make sure to enter the correct data in the correct field. 2. You have paid a too high/too little fee. If you have paid too much (double payment, wrong prescribed fee) we can reimburse the difference to the required fee. → Solution: Submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). If you have paid to little you can pay the remaining difference until the end of the payment period. Make sure to enter the correct payment data. If the fee is not paid fully or in due time your degree programme will be closed. → Solution: Pay the remaining difference before the end of the payment period. 3. You have entered the unique payment reference (called "Semesterkundendaten" before) of a wrong semester If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges

retrieved.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
retrieved.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for third parties
retrieved.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

Possible reasons for errors are: the self-service machine in the bank or the bank assistent did not or did not correctly forward the unique payment reference. If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again (best way is directly in u:space via eps-payment or credit card). Otherwise make sure to enter the correct data. 5. You have paid in cash. If you pay in cash at a bank the payment reference is frequently not forwarded. If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the bank location the payment was made from. → Solution: Directly contact the bank location you paid at. 6. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
reranked.text: #### Teacher Education programme: Change of a teaching subject This kind of change is not possible via u:space. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the [deadline](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/) (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). The Admission Office will effectuate the change within the admissio period, which you will see after a few days in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
reranked.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again. Make sure to enter the correct data in the correct field. 2. You have paid a too high/too little fee. If you have paid too much (double payment, wrong prescribed fee) we can reimburse the difference to the required fee. → Solution: Submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). If you have paid to little you can pay the remaining difference until the end of the payment period. Make sure to enter the correct payment data. If the fee is not paid fully or in due time your degree programme will be closed. → Solution: Pay the remaining difference before the end of the payment period. 3. You have entered the unique payment reference (called "Semesterkundendaten" before) of a wrong semester If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges

reranked.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
reranked.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

After a processing time of few weeks you will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents. 5. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after the results of the entrance exam were announced and you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. The sporting aptitude admission test takes place twice per year. Therefore, an application for admission to the [bachelor programme in Sport and Human Movement Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/) is also possible before the summer semester. Please note the applicable [application and admission deadlines](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/#c490977). - [Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure when changing a degree programme](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/)

reranked.title: Confirmations for third parties
reranked.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

Make sure to enter the latest unique payment reference in the correct field. 4. You have completed the payment form correctly. Nevertheless an error occurred. Possible reasons for errors are: the self-service machine in the bank or the bank assistent did not or did not correctly forward the unique payment reference. If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the account the payment was made from. This return remittance may cause charges that will be deducted from your account! → Solution: Pay the fee again (best way is directly in u:space via eps-payment or credit card). Otherwise make sure to enter the correct data. 5. You have paid in cash. If you pay in cash at a bank the payment reference is frequently not forwarded. If this is the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the bank location the payment was made from. → Solution: Directly contact the bank location you paid at. 6. selected.title: Change of personal data
selected.text:
During your studies, some of your personal data might change (e.g. your address or academic degree). It may also happen that some of your personal data in our system are incorrect (e.g. date of birth, spelling of your name).

- You can change certain data yourself via u:space, while other changes require you to provide documentation.
- Data changes are only made for active students and persons who are in the process of applying for admission.
- Doctoral/PhD students who would like to change their personal data should contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
- If you are attending a university course and would like to change your personal data, please contact the [Service Center of the Postgraduate Cente](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/)r.

You can change the following data yourself via [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast).

- Forwarding e-mail address
- Phone number
- Home and different delivery address
- Bank account details

Please note that some of these fields are required fields. Bear in mind to keep your data up to date.

If your name has changed (e.g. through marriage), please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable documentation (e.g. marriage certificate) and a scan of your passport or ID card. As soon as your has been changed, you can order a new [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal Matters > My u:card.

selected.title: Change of personal data
selected.text:
If you want your academic degree(s) that were awarded by a different university/higher education institution to appear on all your documents, please send the applicable documentation (e.g. official notice of degree award) via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office. We can only enter degrees awarded by academic institutions within the EU/EEA or degrees recognised through a [nostrification](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/) procedure. Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=ssc) of your degree programme, if an academic degree awarded by the University of Vienna does not appear in your personal data in u:space.

If you changed your citizenship, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (proof of citizenship and passport or ID card).

If your gender has been entered incorrectly, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (passport or ID card).

[Information about change of name and person for trans\*persons](http://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/aufgaben/namens-und-personenstandsaenderungen-fuer-transpersonen/)

In case your date of birth is incorrect or your name is misspelled in our system, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing documentation for the correct spelling or date of birth (passport or ID card).

selected.title: Confirmations for (former) students
selected.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

During your studies, some of your personal data might change (e.g. your address or academic degree). It may also happen that some of your personal data in our system are incorrect (e.g. date of birth, spelling of your name).

- You can change certain data yourself via u:space, while other changes require you to provide documentation.
- Data changes are only made for active students and persons who are in the process of applying for admission.
- Doctoral/PhD students who would like to change their personal data should contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

You can change the following data yourself via [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast).

- Forwarding e-mail address
- Phone number
- Home and different delivery address
- Bank account details

Please note that some of these fields are required fields. Bear in mind to keep your data up to date.

If your name has changed (e.g. through marriage), please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable documentation (e.g. marriage certificate) and a scan of your passport or ID card. As soon as your has been changed, you can order a new [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal Matters > My u:card.

[2] Change of personal data
If you want your academic degree(s) that were awarded by a different university/higher education institution to appear on all your documents, please send the applicable documentation (e.g. official notice of degree award) via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office. We can only enter degrees awarded by academic institutions within the EU/EEA or degrees recognised through a [nostrification](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/) procedure. Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=ssc) of your degree programme, if an academic degree awarded by the University of Vienna does not appear in your personal data in u:space.

If you changed your citizenship, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (proof of citizenship and passport or ID card).

If your gender has been entered incorrectly, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (passport or ID card).

[Information about change of name and person for trans\*persons](http://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/aufgaben/namens-und-personenstandsaenderungen-fuer-transpersonen/)

In case your date of birth is incorrect or your name is misspelled in our system, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing documentation for the correct spelling or date of birth (passport or ID card).

[3] Confirmations for (former) students — Confirmations of admission > Info for former students
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

[4] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor programmes with compulsory OSA > Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

[5] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Further bachelor/diploma programmes > Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

[6] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure > Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
#### Teacher Education programme: Change of a teaching subject This kind of change is not possible via u:space. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the [deadline](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/) (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). The Admission Office will effectuate the change within the admissio period, which you will see after a few days in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

6.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Change of personal data
During your studies, some of your personal data might change (e.g. your address or academic degree). It may also happen that some of your personal data in our system are incorrect (e.g. date of birth, spelling of your name).

- You can change certain data yourself via u:space, while other changes require you to provide documentation.
- Data changes are only made for active students and persons who are in the process of applying for admission.
- Doctoral/PhD students who would like to change their personal data should contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
- If you are attending a university course and would like to change your personal data, please contact the [Service Center of the Postgraduate Cente](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/)r.

You can change the following data yourself via [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast).

- Forwarding e-mail address
- Phone number
- Home and different delivery address
- Bank account details

Please note that some of these fields are required fields. Bear in mind to keep your data up to date.

If your name has changed (e.g. through marriage), please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable documentation (e.g. marriage certificate) and a scan of your passport or ID card. As soon as your has been changed, you can order a new [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal Matters > My u:card.

[2] Change of personal data
If you want your academic degree(s) that were awarded by a different university/higher education institution to appear on all your documents, please send the applicable documentation (e.g. official notice of degree award) via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office. We can only enter degrees awarded by academic institutions within the EU/EEA or degrees recognised through a [nostrification](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/) procedure. Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=units&query=ssc) of your degree programme, if an academic degree awarded by the University of Vienna does not appear in your personal data in u:space.

If you changed your citizenship, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (proof of citizenship and passport or ID card).

If your gender has been entered incorrectly, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing the applicable proof (passport or ID card).

[Information about change of name and person for trans\*persons](http://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/aufgaben/namens-und-personenstandsaenderungen-fuer-transpersonen/)

In case your date of birth is incorrect or your name is misspelled in our system, please use the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) (Topic: Change of data) to reach the Admission Office, providing documentation for the correct spelling or date of birth (passport or ID card).

[3] Confirmations for (former) students — Confirmations of admission > Info for former students
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

[4] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor programmes with compulsory OSA > Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). Question: Where can I change my address, phone number or bank details as a student?

generate.raw_response: You can change your address, phone number, or bank account details yourself via u:space [1].

answer: You can change your address, phone number, or bank account details yourself via u:space [1].
